Fern Polrrrcnl PRACTTcEs CourvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
May 6,2010
John H. Pruitt, Jr.
L2597 Jalapeflo Avenue
Chino, Califomia 917 l}l
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-063
Dear Mr. Pruitt:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the reporting provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").' this letter is based o-n the Iacts presentei;'the Fair political
Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
QIJESTION
As an elected member of the Chino Valley Unified School District Board, do you have
any reporting obligations under the Act for tickets you received from the Los Angeles Dodgers
for schoolchildren to attend baseball games at Dodger stadium?
CONCLUSION
While your receipt of these tickets is neither a gift nor a contribution under the Act, as
discussed below, you may be required to report the tickets as a "behested payment,, under section
82015(b)(2XBXiii).
FACTS
You were informed by a friend that the Los Angeles Dodgers provide tickets to public
school students. You mentioned to your friend that yo; thought lhe students of Chino Valley
Unified School District would enjoy attending a game at Dod-ger Stadium. He provided your
name and address to his contact in the Dodger organization. As a school board member, you
received 600 baseball tickets from the Dodgers oiganization.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g 1000 through g l0l4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of thi Calitbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No.A-10-063
Page No. 2
After you received the tickets, you gave them to the school superintendent for distribution
to the students' The. superintendent provided the tickets to school principals for distribution to
the students' You, did not personally use or distribute any of the tickets, ttner than to pass them
along to the superintendent- The only correspondence that came with the tickets was a typed
note of "enclosed are 600 tickets." The deliviry package had your name on it the label. you
would like to know if you have any reporting auties uni.. the Act.
ANALYSIs
The Act defines gift as"any payment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to
the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received . . ..,, (Section g202g.)
Your facts indicate that these tickets were provided as part of the Dodgerr' p.ogru- to make
tickets available to public school students, and not to piovide a personal b.n"fi;;;;";.tfia
personal benefit was received by the students in your school district, who are not subiect to the
provisions of the Act. Accordingly, you did not ieceive a gift from the DodgerJ 
rv r'v
Section 82015 defines a "contribution" as a payment ... except to the extent that full and
adequate consideration is received, unless tt ts ,t"oiliom the ,urrouidirg circ:umstances that it
ts not made for political purposes. " Giyen the facts iresented, including ihe fact that under the
Dodgers program tickets are made available to any sctroot district that s-ubmit a request, we do
not know how the surrounding circumstar"", 
"ouid 
be any clearer that the puv*""t was not
made for a political purpose. Accordingly, you did not releive a contribution, and you have no
reporting obligations under the Act.
However section 8:2015(b)(2XBXiii) under certain circumstances, requires an elected
official to file a report when a person makes a payment at the behest of the official that is neither
a gift nor a contribution but is never the less foi a legislative, governmental or charitable
purpose' under these provisions, when, as here, un offi"iul diiectly or through ;n agent solicits
the donation of something of valuea for use by a government agency such as a public school and
the value of the donation is $5,000 or more in a c-atenaar year, Irr" orn"iut is refiuired to file a
"behested payment" report detailing the donation within iO auy, after the donation is made. This
information must be reported on Form g03, which we enclose.
According to the Dodger organization, tickets may be requested in writing on school district letterhead,
where they are processed as part of their program. Your request ,""rn, t have gotten into the process through a
contact in the Dodger organization ratheithan an official ,"iu.rt in *.iting on d]strict l"ti"rrr.oi. other than that,
the request seems to have been filled in accordance with the prog.umr pori.y and the tickets served the same
purpose' Therefore, we do not see a distinction because or now ire prJ""r, was initiated.
3 while regulation I 8941 (aX3) provides that an official receives a gift when he or she has actual possession
of the gift or exercises direction or control over the gift, regardless oi*t "tti, 
the official turns the gift over to
another person, we think this regulation is not appli&ble uider these facts. Here, the 600 rickets were specifically
SfJt|j::lj*"T'-"rs 
for school children in your school district and distribution were not ,uu1".t to you,
'The term "payment,, includes . . .. (Section g2OM)


File No.A-10-063
Page No. 3
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Y,
.t'S cott Hallabrin
General Counsel
sHjgl
Enclosure
